description_long: This is a vintage, box-shaped record player with a weathered, metallic exterior and a hinged lid that is propped open. The surface is heavily worn, showing patches of rust and scratches, giving it an aged, industrial look. Inside, there is a circular black turntable platter with a visible vinyl record, and a metallic tonearm extending over the platter. The interior also contains a stack of additional records and a few metallic components, possibly for operation or decoration. The front of the box features a protruding handle and reinforced corners, while the overall build appears robust and utilitarian. The color palette is dominated by dark browns, blacks, and metallic grays, with a matte, rough finish.
description: A weathered, metallic vintage record player with an open lid, visible turntable, and vinyl records inside. The exterior is rugged and rusted, with a utilitarian, industrial design.
description_view_0: The back of the record player, showing the open lid and reinforced metallic corners, with no interior details visible.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the open lid, the turntable platter with a vinyl record, and a stack of records inside the box.
description_view_2: The front view displays the open lid, the turntable platter with a record, the tonearm, and the handle on the front panel.
description_view_3: The opposite side view highlights the handle, the open lid, and the turntable platter with the tonearm positioned over it.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and occupies a significant portion of the interior. It is black, likely made of plastic or metal, with a visible vinyl record on top. The platter has a matte finish and subtle grooves, with a yellow label at the center.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and extends over the platter. It is thin, straight, and appears to be made of metal with a dull finish. The tonearm is positioned above the record, suggesting it is ready for playback.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm.
attribute_dust_cover: The dust cover is present as a hinged, metallic lid that is currently open. It is opaque, made of the same weathered metal as the rest of the player, and attached with visible hinges at the back.
